description: Labelled recall set. Queries are paraphrased away from the fact wording so lexical matching is stressed; some facts are hard-negative distractors (no query targets them: 16, 17, 33, 59, 63); many facts carry two or three paraphrase queries; some queries are multi-relevant (relevant lists more than one fact). relevant = indices into facts. Widened in the working-memory-levers work so between-condition Recall deltas of ~0.1 clear the Wilson 95% interval. v2 (2026-07-06): +233 facts mined from the live store (human-reviewed, PII-scrubbed, contamination-filtered) with authored paraphrase queries.
facts: The project deploys to AWS Lambda using the Serverless Framework.
Database migrations are managed with Alembic and run in CI before every deploy.
The API authenticates requests using short-lived JWT access tokens.
Frontend state is handled by Zustand rather than Redux.
Secrets are stored in AWS Secrets Manager and never committed to git.
The team runs a two-week sprint cadence with a demo every other Friday.
Unit tests use pytest and end-to-end tests use Playwright.
The primary database is PostgreSQL 16 hosted on Amazon RDS.
Background jobs are processed by a Celery worker pool backed by Redis.
Arabic is the default locale so layouts must be right-to-left.
Images are optimised and served through a CloudFront CDN.
The mobile app is built with React Native and Expo.
Merging code requires at least one approving review.
Rate limiting is enforced at the API gateway at 100 requests per minute.
Shared UI components live in a package called ui-kit.
Application logs are shipped to Datadog as structured JSON.
The onboarding document explains how to request database credentials.
A quick reference of common shell commands is pinned in the wiki.
The CI pipeline runs on GitHub Actions with a matrix across Python 3.10 to 3.13.
Feature flags are managed through LaunchDarkly.
The design system uses Tailwind CSS with a custom theme.
API responses use cursor-based pagination rather than offset pagination.
The staging environment is redeployed automatically on every merge to main.
Error tracking and alerting are handled by Sentry.
Full-text search is powered by an Elasticsearch cluster.
Transactional email is sent through Amazon SES with a dedicated sending domain.
The codebase is a monorepo managed with Turborepo.
Type checking is enforced in CI with mypy in strict mode.
Container images are built with Docker and pushed to Amazon ECR.
Analytics events are streamed through Kafka into a Snowflake warehouse.
Accessibility is tested with axe and must meet WCAG 2.1 AA.
The public REST API is documented with an OpenAPI spec served at /docs.
Local development spins up its dependencies with Docker Compose.
The support rota is published in the team calendar each month.
Cloud infrastructure is provisioned with Terraform and its state lives in an S3 backend.
The data pipeline is orchestrated with Apache Airflow DAGs.
Machine-learning models are tracked and versioned in MLflow.
The recommendation model is retrained nightly on the previous day's events.
Personally identifiable information is encrypted at rest with AES-256-GCM.
Access to the production environment requires a hardware security key and SSO.
The marketing site is server-side rendered with Next.js.
A GraphQL endpoint is exposed alongside REST for the mobile clients.
WebSocket connections carry real-time notifications to the browser.
The billing service integrates with Stripe for recurring subscriptions.
Customer data is partitioned by tenant within a shared PostgreSQL schema.
Nightly backups are written to a separate AWS account for disaster recovery.
The search index is rebuilt from scratch every Sunday at 02:00 UTC.
Product analytics dashboards are built in Metabase.
The team records architectural choices as written decision records.
Incident retrospectives are blameless and published internally.
Load testing is run with k6 before every major release.
The API version is pinned in the URL path as /v2.
Type stubs for untyped third-party libraries live in a typings directory.
The Kubernetes cluster autoscales worker nodes on CPU and queue depth.
Canary deployments route five percent of traffic before a full rollout.
Secrets are rotated automatically every ninety days.
The onboarding checklist is completed within a new hire's first week.
Documentation is written in Markdown and published with Docusaurus.
Feature branches are deleted automatically once their pull request merges.
The status page is hosted externally and updated during incidents.
Cost dashboards flag any service that exceeds its monthly budget.
The mobile app ships to beta testers through TestFlight.
Direct messages between users are protected with end-to-end encryption.
A weekly newsletter summarises shipped features for stakeholders.
Current semantic path stores model's full native dim with no truncation anywhere; this lever was latent but never exercised
Stronger 2025-generation models exist (EmbeddingGemma-300m, Qwen3-Embedding-0.6B) but require small new adapters in core/adapters/, wired by plugin selector, fail-open to hash per POEAA lock-in
Gateway + Separated Interface seam in core/adapters/ makes adding new embedding models straightforward without core changes
This is the embedding equivalent of 80s/90s packed-pixel formats or fixed-point arithmetic — a resource-constrained optimization that modern hardware enables but data structure doesn't yet use
SessionStart-vs-JIT recall pattern maps to overlay programming / bank switching (small kernel, page-in on demand)
Detached capture mirrors precompute-at-build-time; int8 quantisation mirrors fixed-point arithmetic from resource-constrained eras
Current usage_summary charges full price for SessionStart injection, but it joins cached prefix and re-reads at ~0.1× on every later turn
Measured floor (from get_symbol/get_doc_section/bounded reads with actual byte counts) is conservative; measured-only headline is credible
New module: core/consolidation/scoring.py defines retention_score(fact, now) reusing domain.scoring primitives (recency, frequency, context); pure function
Latency cost of raising cap 150→1500 is negligible (~1ms); token budget is unaffected (gated by min_sim/top_k/max_chars)
Attribution was wired into `recall_structured` (MCP tool, latency-tolerant) but never into `recall_prompt_block` (dominant per-turn path)
Latency budget: one indexed UPDATE + commit on hot path; sub-millisecond in isolation but writes to 5s-ceiling path
Mitigation 3: scope attribution to recall_prompt_block only (JIT, per-turn), NOT recall_core_block (SessionStart, stable, cached)
prune_threshold=0.10 archives facts whose retention score drops below this (score = freq * recency_decay)
Proposal §3 described 'global entity-level conflict merge' and 'gist abstraction'; neither shipped
Sequential Hypothesis's REM contribution (schema integration, clustering related facts) is the gap
New consolidate() stage: cluster near-duplicates, emit entity-level supersedes, abstract gist (LLM-backed, degrades gracefully)
Heuristic dedup ships everywhere; LLM integration available when distiller is configured and present
Atkinson–Shiffrin STM timescale is seconds; cross-session dev memory treats 'short-term' as a state, not a clock
OQ3 = A: Dedicated integrate_threshold config knob, default-off, independent from supersede_threshold for eval tuning
OQ3 decouples near-duplicate-clustering aggressiveness from exact supersession, avoiding operational coupling

Rejected alternative: multiplicative downscale (divide retention_score by frequency factor) — would break reproducibility because comparison sets would have different pass frequencies and different score decays
Clustering algorithm: cosine distance over stored int8 vectors; threshold configurable via integrate_threshold (default 0.0, off; must be < supersede_threshold to avoid semantic collision)
Documentation targets updated: README (CLI list, stats accounting), rules (credit_read.py hook table), generated design doc (removed stale 'not built' claims)
New method signature: merge_cluster(facts, threshold)→(merged_facts, stats)
LTM_INTEGRATE_THRESHOLD=0.92 set for near-identical-only STM→LTM merge
Phase 1 captures mistakes as kind='antipattern' facts via distillation pass, surfaced in recall
PR #16 created: `feat(ltm): anti-pattern catalogue — capture admitted mistakes and prevent repeats`
ltm-design skill is dev-only tooling under .claude/skills/, not shipped in the plugin
3. **#5 (use-feedback inhibition)** — stops steady-state waste; needs a usage signal you don't capture yet.
Q#1 activated_k: chose Option A (add knob defaulting to top_k, decouples activated-LTM breadth from injected focus breadth)
Phase 2: `activated_k` knob added to config, defaults to `top_k` (inert), wired MCP `recall_structured` path only
Phase 3: `render_scaffold` groups core facts by title, structured LT-WM block, cue field added to DistilledFact (inert, no persistence yet)
Phase 4: inhibition term added to retention score (weight 0 default), outcome columns + `mark_injected`/`mark_used` Store methods, pure `inhibition_signal` function
Phase 5: `fact_edges` table + `spread_weight` knob (default 0), pure `spread` and `extract_entities` functions, co-occurrence + shared-entity edges per Option B
PR #21 (6 working-memory levers: dataset widening, gist, focus/activated, scaffold, inhibition, spreading) merged

All 6 phases: inert by default (hot path byte-identical when all off), pure Functional Core for new logic, Repository for persistence, 334 tests (+32 new), migrations upgrade cleanly, no recall regression at baseline
First PR to *enable* (set non-zero weight/config) any lever carries the minor bump
Hot path (search, scoring, injection) remains byte-identical at all-levers-off defaults
Schema migrations (_v14_outcomes, _v15_edges) tested on unchanged behavior
Pure Functional Core (spread, inhibition_signal, extract_entities) is testable and measurable independently
Inert-by-default pattern: store/config additions pay zero cost until lever enabled
PR #20 (ltm-design skill frontmatter fix) merged — ltm-design skill now loads cleanly
Q#5 "used" signal: chose Option B (higher-fidelity multi-signal combining token-reappearance, edit-content, correction-turn, cross-session outcome)
Q#4 edges: chose Option B (co-occurrence AND shared-entity edges upfront, with conservative per-edge weighting to manage false-edge surface)
Default-off discipline gates all phases: code can ship inert until a measured comparison justifies enabling, keeping behavior and tests green
Per-phase commit strategy: each phase gets its own PR with commit message e.g. 'feat(ltm): gist chunking' (type/scope per ltm-git)
No version bump while default-off: bench-only / inert scaffolding = no version bump; only when a lever flips to enabled-by-default does it bump minor
Default-off safety: all levers ship inert/no-op until a measured comparison proves improvement; existing behavior and tests stay green through all phases
Unquoted `: ` in YAML scalar is parsed as invalid mapping key, making entire frontmatter unparseable
Fix restructured description to parenthetical format `claude-ltm (systems designs, ...)`, matching colon-free style of six sibling skills
Skill build checks description length against 1024-char limit and forbids angle brackets `<>`
Build does NOT verify frontmatter parses under yaml.safe_load()
Stronger validation habit would treat 'frontmatter parses as YAML' as primary check, length/`<>` as secondary
PR #19 landed on origin/main as squash commit 64ae312
Ran git pull to sync local main to the new commit
Recall's gate-and-cap machinery *is* an externalised **central executive** deciding what gets into the focus.
4. **#4 (spreading activation)** — precision play; highest complexity/storage cost, so weigh carefully.
SKILL.md spine: 12 non-negotiables, 6 system-research sections, statistical contract, working method (2138 words, 1012-char description)
Assets: paper_template.md (6-section skeleton + pandoc metadata), pandoc/paper.yaml + pandoc/header.tex (LaTeX, no exotic packages)
Script gracefully degrades when toolchain absent: prints brew install guidance, exits 1, leaves sources untouched
CLAUDE.md update (skill enumeration 6→7) is documentation, not plugin code change
Commit type = `docs` signals no version bump (matches precedent 'docs: shape .claude rules/skills…')
Version bump rule: only plugins/ltm/** changes trigger release increments per ltm-git habit
Frontmatter validation: name=folder, description <1024 chars, no <> in description field (angle brackets belong in argument-hint)
Default (no --publish): builds PDF beside source markdown in current directory
Graceful degradation: if pandoc or tectonic absent, prints `brew install pandoc tectonic` guidance, exits 1, leaves source files untouched, creates no PDF
Detection via Python subprocess; no hard dependencies on pandoc/tectonic in the script or codebase
argparse wired for --publish flag; script compiles and passes syntax check
Skill list now: ltm-analyse, ltm-context, ltm-design, ltm-git, ltm-plan, ltm-poeaa, ltm-test (7 total, alphabetical)
Initial description was 1025 chars (1 char over limit); trimmed to 1012 by removing em dash and tightening phrasing
Angle brackets in argument-hint (e.g., <change>, <slug>) are correct and match sibling skills (ltm-analyse, ltm-plan); no-<> rule applies only to description field
OQ2: Build tool is Python build_pdf.py with --publish flag for datetime-stamping, archiving, and root-promotion (not bash); default builds PDF beside source
OQ3: Optional .bib + pandoc-citeproc for academic citations (numbered in-text, auto-formatted refs); plain Markdown ## References section as fallback when .bib absent
Working drafts live in docs/generated/design-drafts/<slug>/ (gitignored); canonical PDF on repo root next to README.md/DESIGN.md
Settings.json**: Update `~/.claude/settings.json` with all the config opt-ins.
PR/release**: Commit and raise PRs on the <personal-account> gh cli account; bump plugin version to 0.14.0; fold per-feature version-bumping into the ltm-git release habit.
claude-ltm**: token-first cross-project long-term memory + code/docs index plugin for Claude Code. Stdlib-only core; opt-in adapters (fastembed, LLM distillers). Local-first.
CQRS + Hexagonal (Ports & Adapters)**: write side (capture, detached) vs read side (recall, hot-path).
POEAA capture-write bundle**: Command/Handler + Functional Core/Imperative Shell + Repository (not Active Record) + Gateway/Separated-Interface + Special-Case/Null-Object.
Distiller Strategy pattern**: HeuristicDistiller (default, stdlib), ClaudeCliDistiller, HTTPDistiller. `summarize`/`merge_cluster`/`extract_antipatterns` return None/[] for heuristic (Special Case).
render_block** injects ONLY `- {row['text']}` (one line per fact); title/subtitle/narrative NOT injected on JIT path.
VSCode `/plugin` commands don't expose marketplace sync in the extension UI; use CLI (`claude plugin marketplace update`) for manual control
Plugin reload updates hooks/deps but does NOT restart any running viewer process
Plugin caching/version was actually up-to-date (0.8.0); the UI appeared outdated only due to stale viewer (PID 10590 from 9:59 PM)
DESIGN.md § Sleep pass: updated to list `integrate` stage alongside replay/displace/refine/purge
core/recall/ is a Python package; the entire implementation lives in core/recall/__init__.py, not redundant
IDEs typically hide __init__.py by default in the file tree, creating a false appearance of emptiness
Package structure intentional: matches POEAA concern-grouping pattern with other core/ subpackages (domain/, ports/, index/)
Documented in .claude/rules/02-architecture/01-poeaa-and-layers.md as 'read side — search/render; from core.recall import …'
Used in service.py (30), hooks, and MCP server; removing or flattening would break imports
Leaves room for future module splits (render, ranking) without breaking import surface
Manifests locked at 0.13.1 from PR #9 through #16 despite multiple feature/fix additions
Local marketplace caches versions; no auto-update means users never received features from PRs #10–#16 without manual /plugin update
Root cause: version-bumping was optional and easy to skip, not a habitual step
Solution: version bump now Step 3 in ltm-git pr-create for feature/fix/perf PR types, keeping marketplace in sync with code changes
Implementation: formalized in PR #18 update to ltm-git skill
plugin.json and marketplace.json both bumped to 0.14.0
ruff format intentionally skipped on JSON (known corruption: adds trailing commas)
Version-only change (no Python code); reflects PR #16 anti-pattern catalogue feature
Step 2: User manually updates plugin to 0.14.0 via /plugin manager (interactive step; cannot be automated)
Step 4: Verify via ltm doctor (antipatterns: line appears) and capture (new code runs, LTM_* env vars read)
Docs/test/chore/ci/.claude PRs skip the version-bump step
Local-directory marketplace requires version bump to activate new features for users
Plugin `/plugin` update is what pulls new code; metadata refresh alone doesn't upgrade cached version
`ruff format --check` scoped to changed files in the PR only
PR #18 is dev-tooling (`.claude/**`), scope `docs`, does not bump version on itself
Plugin version 0.14.0 available in repository but not pulled to user cache
`gh` auth is currently set to user `<personal-account>` (owns <owner>/claude-ltm)
Use: `gh auth switch --user <work-account>`
Plugin versions are cached locally under `~/.claude/plugins/cache/claude-ltm/ltm/<version>/` and must be manually updated via `/plugin` UI
A restart refreshes marketplace metadata but does not bump an already-installed plugin's version number
Repository marketplace is a local directory pointing at this repo, advertising 0.13.1, creating ambiguity when version is static across content changes
Repository <owner>/claude-ltm has no CI configured
Active gh account <work-account> lacks push access to <owner>/claude-ltm (repo owned by <personal-account>)
Local main fast-forwarded successfully; feat/antipattern-catalogue branch deleted and remote pruned
Settings LTM_REFINE_PRUNE_PERCENTILE=0.05 is now safe—Phase 2 exemption shipped with the merge
Full implementation live: Phase 1 (capture as kind='antipattern'), Phase 2 (drift invalidation + archive), Phase 3 (PreToolUse warnings)
Two optional refinements remain deferred: recurrence-reinforce (Q6-C) and LLM applicability judgement (Q4b-C)
Phase 2 invalidates anti-patterns when referenced files disappear (drift signal), preserving reversibility by archiving to 'expired' status
Phase 3 PreToolUse hook warns before Bash/Edit/Write actions match any anti-pattern rule (project or global scope)
Phase 3 matching is lexical-only (≥2 token overlap), ≥600 char capped, deduped per (session, rule) to bound token cost
Store queried only for the four action tools (Bash/Edit/Write/MultiEdit) to minimize cost
Capped to 600 characters per injection; deduped per (session, rule) so each rule warns at most once per session
Lexical token matching only—no embedding on interactive path, fail-open on any error
End-to-end subprocess test confirms hook emits additionalContext and exits 0 under error conditions
Takes priority over search nudges in prefer_memory.py flow
LTM_REFINE_PRUNE_PERCENTILE=0.05 in settings.json entered shell env; test_consolidation.StageTests.setUp built cfg from get_config() and saw ambient value instead of code default
Four consolidation tests failed (test_prepare_stages, test_displace_stm, test_integrate, test_purge_expired) when lever was unset in setUp
Fix: pin all six consolidation levers to code defaults in StageTests.setUp (stm_capacity, keep_max, pct, merge_threshold, purge_horizon, integrate_threshold)
Per-test replace(...) overrides still work after setUp pin
Phase 1–3 covers capture, lifecycle, and prevention—the core loop
Recurrence reinforcement (strengthen fact on each prevention) deferred as optimization
Deferral is intentional, not a blocker; feature is shippable at Phase 3
Anti-pattern exemption (kind='antipattern' guard in refine()) and prune percentile setting are hard-coupled—must land in same commit to avoid pruning anti-patterns on main before exemption ships
Requires Claude Code restart to reach capture/checkpoint hooks
Settings validated via JSON schema check before commit
Retention score is a weighted sum of recency, salience, depth (each [0,1]) plus boosts
Percentile is self-limiting and does not require empirical score distribution data
Absolute floor (keep only facts scoring ≥X) would need empirical distribution to set safely
Percentile semantics ('drop weakest 5%') are intuitive and controllable by the user
Setting affects forgetting across all projects, so the value is fundamentally a user decision
TTL sweep() skips antipatterns (kind-agnostic originally, now exempted)
New invalidate.py module archives project rules when all referenced files are gone (drift signal)
Invalidate wired into consolidate() pipeline before purge stage
invalidate preserves reversibility: archives to 'expired' status, not delete
Conservative scope: archives only when *all* files gone, skips global/fileless rules
LTM promotion (tier='ltm' at capture) already shipped Phase 1, verified in this pass
displace_stm builds on stm_rows query (tier='stm' only)
integrate uses merge_candidates (tier='stm' scoped)
Both skip LTM-tier anti-patterns inherently; no guard needed
get_config() in tests saw LTM_INTEGRATE_THRESHOLD=0.92 from ambient env
Fix: pin integrate_threshold=0 in setUp, matching test_viewer._cfg hermeticity convention
'on' tests already override threshold explicitly, so pinning default is safe
Matches established codebase pattern: stdlib values pinned in test setup
ltm doctor output extended with per-project + global antipattern line counts
Viewer already surfaces anti-patterns as kind='antipattern' facts in fact listing
Dedicated filter facet deferred as polish (not built in Phase 2)
Doctor count visible in `ltm doctor` output; no UI changes needed
Antipattern surfacing scenario: R@3 1.000 (unchanged)
Zero budget impact: all changes in detached consolidation pass (write-side)
Recall unions global antipatterns into search + search_fused; reserved __global__ key routes global-scope facts from all projects into every recall
Dropped the per-session clear_session_kind pattern the initial plan borrowed from the summary distiller
Additive model uses fact_id dedup + supersession as idempotency — matches the catalogue's intent (lessons don't reset)
Prevents a clipped lesson (identified mid-session, then missing from a later extraction pass) from being permanently lost
Aligns with the LTM tier: accumulated facts stay until explicitly invalidated (drift, supersession) or dormancy-exempt Phase 2 skips them
Store.add and add_records both accept tier parameter (not discovered in initial plan sketch)
Anti-patterns can be promoted to LTM immediately at capture time via add_records(tier="ltm")
LTM_ANTIPATTERNS=true added explicitly (already the default, but now visible in config)
Phase 1 builds anti-patterns as LTM-tier facts, but refine() is tier-agnostic (prunes both STM and LTM)
Without Phase 2 exemption (kind="antipattern" skip in refine/displace/purge), anti-patterns can be evicted during consolidation
Phase 2 must ship before turning on LTM_REFINE_PRUNE_PERCENTILE or aggressive forgetting passes
Anti-patterns are currently bounded only by LTM_REFINE_KEEP_MAX=20000 (user is well under this)
Gate markers in Option B reusable later as PreToolUse trigger for Q8 (memory-first enforcement)
Two runtime details flagged for build-time confirmation: reserved global key name ('__global__') and exact injected-text char cap
Q2–Q3: cheap stdlib gate (admission markers) + LLM extraction; injected `text` is Strict Rule with terse DON'T/DO capped to ~180–200 chars
Q4–Q5: hard exemption from dormancy forgetting (but drift-obsolete/superseded still invalidate); scope is LLM-tagged (global reserved key vs project-local)
Q6: feed existing anti-patterns into prompt for LLM-driven dedup/supersession; recurrence-reinforce is fast-follow
Q7: gate+throttle-by-growth cadence (mirror summary distiller); enabled by default with independent config toggle
Q8: two-phase recall delivery — A (JIT UserPromptSubmit, free) then B (PreToolUse lexical/FTS warning, capped+fail-open)
Config toggle pattern: follows existing `Config` dataclass convention; independent flag (e.g., 'antipatterns') added per design Q7b
Re-entrancy backstop: `is_distiller_prompt` method in service layer already guards against capture recursion; anti-pattern extraction extends this
Forgetting exemption: `refine` method in store layer is the point where dormancy logic applies; anti-patterns bypass this for stale-marking only
Q2 gate mechanism suppresses most anti-pattern extractions; throttling by transcript growth keeps cost low even at frequent cadence
Q7a recommendation: gate+throttle-by-growth; reuse maybe_capture_summary throttle mechanism verbatim for consistent cost profile
Extraction validation: stdlib unittest covers gate markers, parse_antipatterns, scope classification, supersede-dedup with stub distiller
Default test path network-free: stub distiller returns known anti-patterns, no live LLM, preserves stdlib-first core contract
Addresses both distinct risks: (1) anti-patterns polluting normal recall precision; (2) anti-patterns not actually working
Tool-usage anti-patterns fire mid-turn on tool calls unrelated to the user's prompt topic, not via prompt-level JIT recall alone
JIT recall (UserPromptSubmit) injects anti-patterns by similarity to prompt; free, already works, covers prompt-topic cases
PreToolUse guard runs right when the model constructs the tool call — the exact place tool-usage mistakes happen
Option B phasing: ship A (JIT-only) first as zero-risk baseline, then B (add PreToolUse lexical/FTS guard) as committed follow-up
PreToolUse anti-pattern lookup must be lexical/FTS only (no embedding), capped, fail-open to preserve hot-path contract
Global anti-patterns (e.g., curl flag rules) surface everywhere; project-scoped rules stay local to origin
Dual-path solves flagship case: tool-usage rules surface where they're needed, not just where the prompt mentions the tool
B allows LLM to emit `supersedes` for true refinements and omit exact repeats
B directly parallels proven main-distiller pattern (pass `existing`, let LLM disambiguate)
Prevents duplicate/churn at source instead of post-facto supersession
Option C (recurrence-reinforce) locked as fast-follow, not MVP blocker
Anti-patterns are rare, keeping prompt expansion bounded and low-cost
Q3 decision locked: Option B — Strict Rule format with terse DON'T/DO in `text` field, capped to ~180–200 chars
Only `text` field reaches injected context; `narrative`/`subtitle` are viewer/structured-recall only
Do/Don't examples in `text` enable lexical/FTS recall on trigger vocabulary (e.g., 'curl', '--sandbox')
Single-line DTO contract preserved; length cap prevents `max_chars` blowout across multiple facts
Root cause and fuller prose examples remain in `subtitle`/`narrative` for viewer context and learning
Q4 decision: Hard exemption + immediate LTM promotion; anti-patterns skip refine/purge/displace and promote to LTM at capture
Removal only via supersession or manual curation, not time-based decay
Anti-patterns are standing rules, not decaying observations; dormancy should not trigger forgetting
Q4b decision: Supersession (A) implemented now; drift-driven obsolescence (B) as RNR mechanism, C (LLM judgement) deferred opt-in
LLM applicability judgement deferred as opt-in to avoid false-positive retirement of hard-won safety lessons
Global anti-patterns (tool/harness mistakes) stored under reserved global key and unioned into recall everywhere
Ambiguous classifications default to `project` to bias against cross-project pollution
Solves the `curl --sandbox` case (harness-level lesson) without polluting unrelated projects with repo-quirks
Option A (always-run LLM) wastes a full LLM call on every checkpoint, but most sessions have no catalogable mistakes

Option B (LLM + heuristic gate): pure stdlib pre-scan checks for admission markers (I mistakenly, that was wrong, my mistake, shouldn't have, user no/don't) to decide whether to invoke expensive LLM extraction
Option B preserves full LLM quality when mistakes detected, skips LLM call on clean sessions, degrades to clean no-op on heuristic installs (stdlib contract preserved)
Option C (heuristic fallback) stores raw admission sentences as degraded facts, but admissions are not imperative rules and pollute recall with non-actionable noise
Main distiller prompt explicitly suppresses admissions/corrections/chatter — bolting detection onto it would fight core design
I mistakenly put a Bash-tool flag into the curl command. Let me re-prepare and PUT cleanly (sandbox disabled via the tool parameter, not a curl flag).
Phase 1: LTM_DISABLE env flag honored by all 8 hooks; set on distiller subprocess to prevent recursive capture
Phase 1: capture_text backstop detects and drops distiller-prompt transcripts, defensive against nested session escape
Phase 2: get_bus drains pending inproc work_queue to NATS on backend switch; idempotent via msg_id republish
Phase 2: bus_dead_after config knob (~7d default) gates max-age dead-letter sweep; wired previously-uncalled reclaim_expired into capture worker
Phase 3: ltm queue CLI lists work items (all/pending/dead breakdown) and purges (--purge-dead/--purge-stage/--purge-all); top-level backend-neutral
Phase 3: viewer RnR header displays dead-letter count from store.dead_stale query
Production recursion confirmed by forensics: 38 stuck items contained verbatim distiller-prompt strings (only source: headless claude -p capture)
No LTM_DISABLE or re-entrancy guard exists in codebase (grep verified)
ClaudeCliDistiller._complete() at distill.py:465 spawns subprocess with no env= override, inheriting ltm plugin
get_bus() at membus.py:83-96 switches to NatsBus without draining inproc work_queue
Inproc queue is the real accumulation point (38 items stranded during NATS fallback)
One-way inproc→NATS drain is idempotent via msg_id republish, cheap to implement
Default None (mirrors existing `summarize` pattern for optional distiller methods)
Both OpenAI and Claude distillers implement merge_cluster with prompt→parser flow

queries.q: how is the service shipped to production
what runtime hosts our functions in production
what keeps track of schema changes over time
what applies incremental database schema updates
how do we verify who is calling the api
how are api sessions kept stateless and secure
which library holds client-side ui state
where do we keep credentials and api keys
how frequently does the team show progress
how long is an iteration and when is the demo
what tool do we use for browser end-to-end checks
what is the main data store
which managed relational service backs the app
how are long-running tasks handled off the request path
what runs asynchronous work using a redis-backed queue
which text direction should the interface use
does the ui need to mirror for a right-to-left language
how are static assets delivered quickly worldwide
what serves images fast from edge locations
what framework powers the phone application
what is needed before code can be merged
how do we stop clients from overwhelming the endpoints
what caps how many calls a client can make each minute
where do continuous integration jobs execute
how do we toggle features without a redeploy
what handles styling and theming
what utility framework styles the components
how do clients page through large result sets

stm_scenario.description: Measures the stm_recall_weight lever end-to-end. Each fresh STM fact (odd index) has an older LTM competitor (the preceding even index) that shares the query's vocabulary. As stm_recall_weight falls from 1.0 toward 0, the STM gold facts must lose rank to their LTM competitors — a monotonic demonstration that the tier lever is measurable. ltm_indices marks the facts promoted to the long-term tier; the rest stay short-term. relevant = indices into stm_scenario.facts.
facts: The service deploys to AWS Lambda using the Serverless Framework.
The service now deploys to Google Cloud Run using containers.
The primary datastore is PostgreSQL hosted on Amazon RDS.
The primary datastore is now CockroachDB for multi-region writes.
Background jobs run on a Celery worker pool backed by Redis.
Background jobs now run on Temporal durable workflows.

queries.q: where does the service deploy
what is the primary datastore
how do background jobs run

antipattern_scenario.description: Measures that globally-scoped anti-patterns surface cross-project via the recall union. The anti-patterns are stored under the reserved global key; recall runs from a DIFFERENT project whose only local facts are distractors, so a hit proves the union + retrieval works. Queries paraphrase the situation that should trigger each rule. relevant = indices into antipattern_scenario.antipatterns.
antipatterns: Never put an AI-tool flag like --sandbox into a curl shell command; set it in the tool call schema instead
Never push directly to main; branch first and open a pull request
Never hand-edit a generated file; change the generator and regenerate it
distractors: The recall hot path scans int8 vectors with a brute-force cosine.
Capture runs in a detached worker spawned at SessionEnd.
The viewer is a localhost stdlib http.server.

queries.q: how do I pass a sandbox disabled option to a curl request
adding a tool sandbox flag onto the shell command line
can I commit straight onto the main branch
I want to edit the generated output file by hand

duplicate_cluster_scenario.description: Measures the integrate (gist-chunking) stage — Idea #3. The first three facts are a near-duplicate cluster (same content, reworded); the rest are distinct. After the heuristic integrate floor runs at the given threshold, the cluster must collapse to one survivor (active count drops) while recall of the cluster's answer is preserved (a query for that content still hits the survivor). relevant = indices into duplicate_cluster_scenario.facts; threshold is the cosine merge floor to test.
facts: The primary database is PostgreSQL 16 hosted on Amazon RDS.
The primary database is PostgreSQL 16 running on Amazon RDS.
The primary datastore is PostgreSQL 16 on Amazon RDS.
Background jobs are processed by a Celery worker pool backed by Redis.
Arabic is the default locale so layouts must be right-to-left.
Application logs are shipped to Datadog as structured JSON.

queries.q: what is the primary database
how are background jobs processed
